title: 2026 World Cup national team profile: The injury storm of the Dutch national team
original_text:
2026 World Cup national team profile: The injury storm of the Dutch national team
Overview
The Dutch national team enters the 2026 World Cup in an unideal context when coach Ronald Koeman faces a serious injury crisis. A series of important pillars of Oranje have physical problems in the preparation phase, making the process of building the squad more difficult than ever.
Names like Xavi Simons, Jerdy Schouten, Matthijs de Ligt, Frenkie de Jong, Denzel Dumfries or Memphis Depay have all had to be sidelined for a long time or have not reached their best condition before the tournament. This forced Koeman to adjust his plans and consider more personnel options than initially expected.
However, the Netherlands still sets very high goals. The Dutch Football Federation wants the team to at least reach the semi-finals, while Koeman publicly expressed his ambition to compete for the world championship.
As a believer of Johan Cruyff's football philosophy, Koeman always prioritizes attacking play and ball control. However, the current force situation may force him to be more flexible instead of being absolutely loyal to the traditional 4-3-3 formation.
Oranje is in Group F with Japan, Sweden and Tunisia - a group considered very unpredictable. Therefore, physical fitness and stability can be put first instead of experiments imbued with total football identity.
Koeman wants his team to play with high intensity, clear personality and always maintain confidence against all opponents. With the depth of force still belonging to the strong group of Europe, the Netherlands is expected to be a formidable candidate in North America.
Coach
Ronald Koeman is one of the most influential figures in Dutch football history. He has achieved success as a player and coach at Ajax, PSV and Feyenoord.
Since returning to lead the national team, Koeman has always received great respect from the players thanks to his experience and prestige. However, the 2026 World Cup is also a challenging period for the 63-year-old strategist when he has to both solve the force problem and accompany his wife Bartina in the fight against cancer.
In that special circumstance, Koeman still holds the leading role, directing Oranje towards the goal of conquering the first world title in history.
Star
Virgil van Dijk continues to be the number one leader of the Dutch national team.
The Liverpool center-back is not only an important stopper in the defense but also plays a bridge between the coaching staff and the players. Experience, bravery and defense command ability help Van Dijk become an irreplaceable factor for Oranje.
At the age of 33, he is still considered one of the best center-backs in the world and the biggest support for the Netherlands' ambitions.
Notable faces
Micky van de Ven is emerging as one of the most watched defenders of the Netherlands.
The Tottenham star possesses superior speed, strong tackling ability and impressive fitness. After many years of trying to assert himself from Volendam to Wolfsburg and then the Premier League, Van de Ven finally won an important position in Koeman's plan.
The 2026 World Cup is considered an opportunity for him to assert his class on the biggest stage.
The silent hero
Frenkie de Jong may no longer be a name that receives as much attention as before, but his role is still extremely important in the Netherlands' system.
The Barcelona midfielder brings the ability to control the pace of the match, escape pressing and circulate the ball that very few players in the Oranje squad can replace. If he reaches the best physical condition, De Jong will be the key to helping the Netherlands realize their ambition to go deep at the 2026 World Cup.
